verdict.label: Go bodyboarding
verdict.headline: Go bodyboarding: Rey Cortiço Mid-morning — 11:00
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço looks like the best bodyboard option for mid-morning — 11:00.  This should feel like a fun progression session rather than a heavy one.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

best.windFit: SW 0 km/h · glassy · good wind fit
best.bestWind: ideal E/ENE/ESE, usable NE/SE, clean <=22 km/h

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, High confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 70 kJ, 9 s period, workable consistency 52/100, and glassy conditions.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at mid tide can turn Belgas punchy and rippable, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Expect: scan from the dunes first because banks and channels shift fast.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

ranked.explanation: Supertubos looks like a clean fun-sized window for Heavy / advanced, Medium confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 55 kJ, 9 s period, lully 38/100, and glassy conditions.  A fast, hollow sand-bottom wave that gets serious quickly; this is best for short, punchy bodyboard barrels when it is small enough for comfort.  Watch: 6-9s at mid tide stays weak and soft for Supertubos / Molhe Leste; Supertubos is a check-first bodyboard size.
ranked.shortExplanation: Supertubos is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide is helping, but the spot still looks serious.
warnings: 6-9s at mid tide stays weak and soft for Supertubos / Molhe Leste
Supertubos is a check-first bodyboard size

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a clean fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Low confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 51 kJ, 14 s period, lully 21/100, and glassy conditions.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 14s+ at mid tide still tends to close out on exposed beaches, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; 14s+ at mid tide still tends to close out on exposed beaches.
ranked.shortExplanation: Almagreira is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide makes this one less convincing.
warnings: 14s+ at mid tide still tends to close out on exposed beaches

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a clean fun-sized window for Too soft, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 51 kJ, 9 s period, lully 40/100, and glassy conditions.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro may feel too soft without more push
